app.title: AutoHydro Argentina
app.subtitle: Smart hydrology for Argentine engineers
app.tagline: Professional hydrological calculations for civil works in Argentina

calculator.title: Hydrological Calculator
calculator.locationPlaceholder: E.g.: Las Flores creek, Chascomús district
calculator.modified_rational_desc: For medium basins (5–50 km²). Includes areal reduction factor.
calculator.scs_cn_desc: Curve Number based. For any basin size.
calculator.tcInfo: Select one or more formulas. The average will be used for design.
calculator.cnHint: Calculated automatically from land use and soil group
calculator.projectNamePlaceholder: E.g.: Hydraulic design for industrial plant access

results.title: Hydrological Analysis Results
results.comparisonDisclaimer: Values from different methods are indicative. The selected method is recommended for the entered basin characteristics.
results.disclaimer: Notice: These results are estimates for preliminary stages. Verify with updated local studies for final designs.

validation.maxOne: Must be a value between 0 and 1
validation.cnCategoriesRequired: Add at least one land use category

errors.calculationFailed: Calculation error. Please verify the entered data.
errors.interpretationFailed: AI interpretation unavailable. The result is still valid.
errors.reportFailed: Error generating PDF. Please try again.
errors.networkError: Connection error. Verify the server is running.

home.heroSubtitle: Calculate design discharges using updated Argentine methodology, AI interpretation, and automatic generation of calculation reports.
home.feature1Desc: IDF coefficients for 15 Argentine cities based on Caamaño Nelli and INA/SMN sources.
home.feature2Desc: Rational, Modified Rational, and SCS-CN with automatic result comparison.
home.feature3Desc: Claude API analyzes your results and generates technical explanations in Spanish.
home.feature4Desc: Automatically generates a calculation report in Argentine technical format ready to submit.
home.disclaimerText: This tool generates estimates for preliminary design stages. The included IDF coefficients are indicative. For final designs, always verify with the most recent local hydrological studies.

about.title: About AutoHydro Argentina
about.developerBio: Civil engineer specialized in hydrology and hydraulics. AutoHydro Argentina was developed to streamline workflows for Argentine civil engineers in hydrological studies, combining rigorous technical methodology with artificial intelligence.
about.methodologyText: IDF curves are based on Caamaño Nelli et al. (1999) regionalization and INA (National Water Institute) publications. Calculation methods follow established Argentine engineering practice.
about.ref1: Caamaño Nelli, G. M. et al. (1999). IDF curve formulas. Ingeniería del Agua.
about.ref2: INA — Instituto Nacional del Agua. Regional technical publications.
about.ref3: USDA-SCS (1972). National Engineering Handbook, Section 4: Hydrology.
about.ref4: Témez, J.R. (1978). Hydrometeorological calculation of peak flows.

footer.tagline: AutoHydro Argentina — Developed by Ing. Ammar Mahfoud
footer.rights: Hydrological estimation tool for professional use
